channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: PaddleOCR 3.5: Running OCR and Document Parsing Tasks with a Transformers Backend
The Open Agent Leaderboard
⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the familiar ransom claim: the data was returned and deleted. The pattern is clear. One weak dependency can...

item.title: The Next War Is Already Here. The West Isn't Ready. — Yaroslav Azhnyuk, The Fourth Law & Guest Host Noah Smith, Noahpinion
item.description: Ukrainian drone founder Yaroslav Azhnyuk went from pet cameras to AI-guided weapons. He and guest host Noah Smith make the case that the West is asleep at the wheel.

item.title: How to Reduce Phishing Exposure Before It Turns into Business Disruption
item.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks that leave teams unsure what was exposed, who else was targeted, and how far the risk has spread....

item.title: Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches
item.description: Several healthcare data breaches impacting hundreds of thousands and even millions were added to the HHS tracker. The post Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ‘Claw Chain’ OpenClaw Flaws Allow Sandbox Escape, Backdoor Delivery
item.description: Four vulnerabilities in OpenClaw can be chained together to steal credentials, escape the sandbox, and plant persistent backdoors. The post ‘Claw Chain’ OpenClaw Flaws Allow Sandbox Escape, Backdoor Delivery appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: Musk v. Altman week 3, and Trump’s tech trading
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Musk v. Altman week 3: Musk and Altman traded blows over each other’s credibility. Now the jury will pick a side. In the final week of the Musk v....

item.title: 7-Eleven Data Breach Confirmed After ShinyHunters Ransom Demand
item.description: The hackers claimed to have stolen more than 600,000 Salesforce records, including personal information and corporate data. The post 7-Eleven Data Breach Confirmed After ShinyHunters Ransom Demand appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
item.description: Supply chain attackers are not only trying to slip malicious code into trusted software. They are trying to steal the access that makes trusted software possible. Recently, three separate campaigns hit npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub in a 48-hour window, and all three targeted secrets from developer...

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
item.description: Ivanti, Fortinet, n8n, SAP, and VMware have released security fixes for various vulnerabilities that could be exploited by bad actors to bypass authentication and execute arbitrary code. Topping the list is a critical flaw impacting Ivanti Xtraction (CVE-2026-8043, CVSS score: 9.6) that could be...

item.title: China Telecom Launches Token Subscription Plans from $1.4/Month, Entering the Token Economy
item.description: China Telecom rolls out its first batch of trial commercial Token plans on World Telecom Day, May 17, with personal plans starting at 9.9 CNY per month for 10 million tokens.

item.title: Shanghai Mobile Launches 5G-A Super Uplink at Scale: 1Gbps Peak Upload in City Core
item.description: Shanghai Mobile announced the large-scale commercial deployment of 5G-A Super Uplink capability on May 17, delivering peak uplink speeds of 1Gbps in core urban areas.

item.title: Hypershell Raises $50M in Series B+ Round, Becomes World's Top-Selling Consumer Exoskeleton
item.description: Hypershell, a Shanghai-based consumer exoskeleton startup founded in 2021, has closed a $50 million Series B+ funding round, cementing its position as the world's top-selling consumer exoskeleton brand by volume.

item.title: Chinese Server CPUs Approach 5GHz Milestone as Intel Supply Crunch Opens Market Window
item.description: Chinese domestic server CPUs are on the cusp of reaching 5GHz clock speeds as Intel's supply constraints and price hikes create a widening opportunity for Hygon and other domestic chipmakers.

item.title: Chinese Surgical Robots Surpass Da Vinci in Domestic Market Share, Expanding into Global Remote Surgery
item.description: China's homegrown surgical robot industry has reached a turning point: the MicroPort MedBot Toumai has overtaken Da Vinci in domestic market share for January–May 2026, while pioneering remote surgery across multiple countries.

item.title: MiniPlasma Windows 0-Day Enables SYSTEM Privilege Escalation on Fully Patched Systems
item.description: Chaotic Eclipse, the security researcher behind the recently disclosed Windows flaws, YellowKey and GreenPlasma, has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day flaw that grants attackers SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems. Codenamed MiniPlasma, the...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: May 18, 07:28 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 18, 06:12 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins
item.description: The flaw leads to denial-of-service on default configurations and to remote code execution if ASLR is disabled. The post Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Issues with Cloudflare Log Explorer
item.description: May 18, 06:33 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 18, 05:57 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 18, 05:37 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue with Cloudflare Log Explorer. Affected customers may see delays...

item.title: Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
item.description: Participants demonstrated exploits for Windows, Linux, VMware, Nvidia, and AI products. The post Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: DeepSlide: From Artifacts to Presentation Delivery
item.description: arXiv:2605.15202v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Presentations are a primary medium for scholarly communication, yet most AI slide generators optimize the artifact (a visually plausible deck) while under-optimizing the delivery process (pacing, narrative, and presentation preparation). We present...

item.title: SDOF: Taming the Alignment Tax in Multi-Agent Orchestration with State-Constrained Dispatch
item.description: arXiv:2605.15204v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent orchestration frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, and CrewAI route tasks through graph-based pipelines but do not enforce the stage constraints that govern real business processes. We present SDOF, a framework that treats multi-agent...

item.title: Does Theory of Mind Improvement Really Benefit Human-AI Interactions? Empirical Findings from Interactive Evaluations
item.description: arXiv:2605.15205v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Improving the Theory of Mind (ToM) capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) is crucial for effective social interactions between these AI models and humans. However, the existing benchmarks often measure ToM capability improvement through...

item.title: SkillSmith: Compiling Agent Skills into Boundary-Guided Runtime Interfaces
item.description: arXiv:2605.15215v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recently, skills have been widely adopted in large language model (LLM)-based agent systems across various domains. In existing frameworks, skills are typically injected into the agent reasoning loop as contextual guidance once matched to a runtime...

item.title: Fair outputs, Biased Internals: Causal Potency and Asymmetry of Latent Bias in LLMs for High-Stakes Decisions
item.description: arXiv:2605.15217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Instruction-tuned language models exhibit behavioural fairness in high-stakes decisions while retaining biased associations in their internal representations. However, whether these suppressed representations can affect model outputs - and whether...

item.title: AgentStop: Terminating Local AI Agents Early to Save Energy in Consumer Devices
item.description: arXiv:2605.15206v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous agents powered by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to automate complex, multi-step tasks such as coding or web-based question answering. While remote, cloud-based agents offer scalability and ease of deployment, they raise...

item.title: TeamTR: Trust-Region Fine-Tuning for Multi-Agent LLM Coordination
item.description: arXiv:2605.15207v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent LLM systems have shown promise for complex reasoning, yet recent evaluations reveal they often underperform single-model baselines. We identify a structural failure mode in sequential fine-tuning of shared-context teams: updating one agent...

item.title: Quantization Undoes Alignment: Bias Emergence in Compressed LLMs Across Models and Precision Levels
item.description: arXiv:2605.15208v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models are routinely compressed via post-training quantization to reduce inference costs and memory footprint for cloud and edge deployment, yet the impact of this compression on model quality remains poorly understood. Existing studies...

item.title: Mask-Morph Graph U-Net: A Generalisable Mesh-Based Surrogate for Crashworthiness Field Prediction under Large Geometric Variation
item.description: arXiv:2605.15231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Nonlinear finite element crash simulations are accurate but computationally expensive, limiting their use in iterative design optimisation. Machine-learning surrogate models based on graph neural networks (GNNs) offer a faster alternative....

item.title: MuteBench: Modality Unavailability Tolerance Evaluation for Incomplete Multimodal Fusion
item.description: arXiv:2605.15235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal physiological data powers clinical AI systems from intensive care units to wearable devices, but sensors routinely fail in practice. Two failure modes are common: modality missing, where an entire channel is absent, and within-modality...

item.title: Always Learning, Always Mixing: Efficient and Simple Data Mixing All The Time
item.description: arXiv:2605.15220v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data mixing decides how to combine different sources or types of data and is a consequential problem throughout language model training. In pretraining, data composition is a key determinant of model quality; in continual learning and adaptation, it...

item.title: Fluency and Faithfulness in Human and Machine Literary Translation
item.description: arXiv:2605.15282v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Literary translation requires balancing target-language fluency with faithfulness to the source. Recent large language models (LLMs) often produce fluent translations, but it remains unclear whether fluency corresponds to semantic preservation in...

item.title: DiscoExplorer: An Open Interface for the Study of Multilingual Discourse Relations
item.description: arXiv:2605.15304v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The relations connecting propositions in discourse such as cause (A because B) or concession (A although B) are a subject of intense interest in Computational Linguistics and Pragmatics, but challenging to study and compare across languages. Recent...

item.title: Automatic Construction of a Legal Citation Graph from 100 Million Ukrainian Court Decisions: Large-Scale Extraction, Topological Analysis, and Ontology-Driven Clustering
item.description: arXiv:2605.15362v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Half a billion citation edges extracted from 100.7 million Ukrainian court decisions reveal that judicial citation structure encodes legal domain boundaries without supervision and predicts future legislative importance with near-perfect accuracy. We...

item.title: Greedy or not, here I come: Language production under vocabulary constraints in humans and resource-rational models
item.description: arXiv:2605.15365v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Communicating using only a limited vocabulary is a common but challenging cognitive phenomenon, requiring an ideal communicator to plan carefully to optimize for intelligibility while circumventing a constrained lexicon. In this work, we investigate...

item.title: PayPal Billing Issues
item.description: May 16, 19:13 UTCIdentified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 16, 17:54 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating issues paying for products with PayPal. Customers may experience an error when attempting to purchase or renew a product...

item.title: Elevated error rates on requests to multiple models
item.description: May 16, 18:24 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 16, 18:18 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 16, 18:08 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Latest open artifacts (#21): Open model bonanza! Gemma 4, DeepSeek V4, Kimi K2.6, MiMo 2.5, GLM-5.1 & others. On CAISI's V4 assessment.
item.description: An eventful month with one flagship release after another

item.title: [AINews] Cerebras' $60B IPO: Slowly, then All at Once
item.description: Congrats Big Chip!

item.title: R2 and Durable Objects increased errors in Eastern North America
item.description: May 16, 02:27 UTCMonitoring - The increase in errors are resolved and the services are stable again.Customers using Queues, Cache Reserve and Cache Purge API may have also been impacted.May 16, 02:07 UTCUpdate - Customers may also see impact with Worker uploads with R2 bindings and when creating...

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 3: Musk and Altman traded blows over each other’s credibility. Now the jury will pick a side.
item.description: In the final week of the Musk v. Altman trial, lawyers traded blows over Elon Musk’s and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s credibility. Altman was grilled on his alleged history of lying and self-dealing involving companies that do business with OpenAI. But he fired back, painting Musk as a power-seeker who...

item.title: Bot Management Issues
item.description: May 15, 16:55 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 15, 14:57 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with our Bot Management feature. Customers may experience an increase in the percentage of traffic detected as automated. We are working to...

item.title: GPT5.5 Performance Degradation
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for reports of GPT 5.5 performing worse for some users.

item.title: Cloudflare Account Level WAF Rule Editing Restricted
item.description: May 15, 14:10 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 15, 14:06 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with editing existing Account level WAF rules. Zone level and new rules are not impacted. Rule operation is not impacted.

item.title: The Download: China’s AI drama factory and the WHO’s missing health targets
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How Chinese short dramas became AI content machines China’s short drama industry is fueled by bite-sized, melodramatic, and smutty shows built for...

item.title: Thinking carefully before adopting agentic AI
item.description: When it comes to using agentic AI, make sure you can walk before you run.

item.title: Learning Context-conditioned Gaussian Overbounds for Convolution-Based Uncertainty Propagation
item.description: Uncertainty quantification is essential in safety-critical settings--from autonomous driving to aviation, finance, and health--where decisions must rely on conservative bounds rather than point estimates. Predictor-level intervals (e.g., from quantile regression, conformal prediction, variance...

item.title: The world is on track to miss its health targets
item.description: Every year the World Health Organization publishes a global health statistics report. It features the numbers behind world health trends and, importantly, assesses whether we’re on track to reach ambitious goals set in 2015. It’s a bit like a health grade. The 2026 report was published on...

item.title: How Chinese short dramas became AI content machines
item.description: In a dimly lit bedroom, a frightened young woman is thrown onto a bed by a tall, muscular man. He grabs her hand, and flame-like vines crawl across her body, fusing with her flesh. She levitates, then drops. A dragon-shaped tattoo appears across her chest. “Two months,” the man says. “Give me an...
